page.title: Marbella Boat Rental Cost Per Person: Shared Charter Prices

data.meta_description: Discover the real Marbella boat rental cost per person for shared charters in Puerto Banús. From €83/person/hour with drinks & skipper included. Book now.
data.summary: This page breaks down the Marbella boat rental cost per person for shared charters, comparing hourly and full-day rates for boats departing Puerto Banús. It covers inclusions like skipper, fuel, and drinks, plus tips for splitting costs with your group.
key_facts: Tier B Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax): €4,719 min 4h, includes free jet ski
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance & 21% VAT
Departures from Puerto Banús, Marbella's luxury marina
No hidden extras – price per person drops as group size increases

faq.q: What is the Marbella boat rental cost per person for a shared charter?
faq.a: For a Tier A boat like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, the cost per person starts at €83 for a 2-hour trip if you fill the boat (9-11 pax). For an 8-hour day, it works out to €209-€255 per person. Larger boats like the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) cost from €393 per person for a 4-hour charter.

faq.q: Does the price per person include skipper and fuel?
faq.a: Yes, every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT. You also get water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There are no extra charges for the crew or fuel – the price you see is the price you pay.

faq.q: How many people can share a boat to get the best per-person rate?
faq.a: The best per-person rates come when you fill the boat. For example, the Azimut 39 takes 11 passengers, so a 2-hour charter at €749 costs just €68 per person. The Mangusta 80 (12 pax) at €4,719 for 4 hours works out to €393 per person.

faq.q: Are shared charters available for half-day or full-day trips?
faq.a: Yes, you can book from 2 hours up to 8 hours. Tier A boats offer a 2-hour option (€749) and an 8-hour option (€2,299). The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking (€4,719). For other boats, pricing is on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on a shared charter?
faq.a: Yes, you're welcome to bring your own food and drinks. The boat already provides water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. If you want to bring a special bottle or a picnic, that's fine – just let the skipper know.

faq.q: What is the cancellation policy for shared boat charters in Marbella?
faq.a: Cancellation policies vary by boat and season. Generally, we require 48-72 hours' notice for a full refund. For last-minute cancellations, a partial refund or rescheduling may be possible. Contact us via WhatsApp for the specific policy for your chosen boat.

faq.q: Which is the cheapest boat per person for a group of 10?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 (11 pax) at €749 for 2 hours gives the lowest per-person cost at €68 per person for a full group. For a longer day, the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) at €2,299 for 8 hours works out to €255 per person – great value for a full-day private charter.

faq.q: Do I need to pay a deposit to secure a shared charter booking?
faq.a: Yes, a deposit is typically required to confirm your booking, with the balance due on the day of departure. The exact deposit amount depends on the boat and season. Contact us via WhatsApp for details on your preferred vessel.

data.body_html:
If you're planning a trip to the Costa del Sol and want to experience the Mediterranean from the water without breaking the bank, understanding the Marbella boat rental cost per person is the first step. Shared charters are the most affordable way to enjoy a luxury yacht, with prices starting at just €83 per person for a 2-hour cruise from Puerto Banús. This page breaks down exactly what you get for your money, how group size affects the price, and which boats offer the best value for your party.

How the Marbella Boat Rental Cost Per Person Works

Our pricing is transparent: the cost per person depends on the boat you choose and how many people share it. All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's iconic luxury marina, and include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT. There are no hidden fees. The price you see is the price you pay – just divide it by your group size to get the per-person cost.

Shared Charter Pricing Tiers

We offer two main pricing tiers for our fleet. Tier A boats are ideal for smaller groups wanting a shorter trip, while Tier B boats cater to larger parties looking for a full-day experience with extras like a jet ski.

  • Tier A: Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – from €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours.
  • Tier B: Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – minimum 4-hour charter at €4,719, including a free jet ski.
  • Other boats: For vessels like the Ferretti 94, Canados 86, or Azimut 58 Flybridge, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp.

Cost Per Person Examples

Here's how the Marbella boat rental cost per person breaks down for real scenarios:

  • Azimut 39 (11 pax), 2-hour charter (€749): €68 per person – the cheapest option per head.
  • Astondoa 40 (9 pax), 8-hour charter (€2,299): €255 per person – a full day on the water with lunch included.
  • Mangusta 80 (12 pax), 4-hour charter (€4,719): €393 per person – includes a jet ski and plenty of space to lounge.

These rates assume the boat is filled to its maximum passenger capacity. Smaller groups will pay a higher per-person cost, but you can always invite friends or book a private charter for just your party.

What's Included in the Price Per Person?

Every shared charter includes the following, so you don't need to worry about extras:

  • Licensed skipper (speaks English and Spanish)
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (crisps, olives, nuts, etc.)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21% VAT)

You can bring your own food and additional drinks if you like – just let the skipper know. The boat also has a cooler and ice.

Popular Destinations on a Shared Charter

From Puerto Banús, you can cruise to several stunning spots along the coast. Most charters head east towards Marbella's Golden Mile and the iconic La Concha mountain, or west to the secluded coves of Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés near Estepona. For a longer trip, you can sail to Sotogrande or even Cabopino. Your skipper will tailor the route to your group's preferences.

How to Book and Save on Per-Person Costs

To get the best Marbella boat rental cost per person, book with a full group. If you're a smaller party, consider joining a shared charter with other travellers – though we don't offer open-join trips, you can ask friends or use social media to fill the boat. For the most accurate pricing and availability, contact us via WhatsApp. We'll confirm the boat, date, and total cost, then you can split it among your group.

For a full overview of our fleet and prices, visit our main page.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the Marbella boat rental cost per person for a shared charter?

For a Tier A boat like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, the cost per person starts at €83 for a 2-hour trip if you fill the boat (9-11 pax). For an 8-hour day, it works out to €209-€255 per person. Larger boats like the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) cost from €393 per person for a 4-hour charter.

Does the price per person include skipper and fuel?

Yes, every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT. You also get water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There are no extra charges for the crew or fuel – the price you see is the price you pay.

How many people can share a boat to get the best per-person rate?

The best per-person rates come when you fill the boat. For example, the Azimut 39 takes 11 passengers, so a 2-hour charter at €749 costs just €68 per person. The Mangusta 80 (12 pax) at €4,719 for 4 hours works out to €393 per person.

Are shared charters available for half-day or full-day trips?

Yes, you can book from 2 hours up to 8 hours. Tier A boats offer a 2-hour option (€749) and an 8-hour option (€2,299). The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking (€4,719). For other boats, pricing is on request via WhatsApp.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on a shared charter?

Yes, you're welcome to bring your own food and drinks. The boat already provides water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. If you want to bring a special bottle or a picnic, that's fine – just let the skipper know.

What is the cancellation policy for shared boat charters in Marbella?

Cancellation policies vary by boat and season. Generally, we require 48-72 hours' notice for a full refund. For last-minute cancellations, a partial refund or rescheduling may be possible. Contact us via WhatsApp for the specific policy for your chosen boat.

Which is the cheapest boat per person for a group of 10?

The Azimut 39 (11 pax) at €749 for 2 hours gives the lowest per-person cost at €68 per person for a full group. For a longer day, the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) at €2,299 for 8 hours works out to €255 per person – great value for a full-day private charter.

Do I need to pay a deposit to secure a shared charter booking?

Yes, a deposit is typically required to confirm your booking, with the balance due on the day of departure. The exact deposit amount depends on the boat and season. Contact us via WhatsApp for details on your preferred vessel.

Ready to book your shared charter? Check out our full fleet and pricing or send us a WhatsApp message to get a quote for your group.
